Title: Water evaporation from soil
Description: Water evaporation from soil

Title: Evaporation from canopy
Description: Evaporation from canopy

Title: Ground heat flux at 3hr
Description: Ground heat flux at 3hr

Title: Total Runoff
Description: the total runoff (including "drainage" through the base of the soil model) leaving the land portion of the grid cell divided by the land area in the grid cell, averaged over the 3-hour interval.

Title: Surface Runoff
Description: surface_runoff_flux

Title: Soil moisture in the top 1 m of the soil column
Description: Soil moisture at 3hr but for 0-1m

Title: Moisture in Upper Portion of Soil Column
Description: the mass of water in all phases in a thin surface soil layer.

Title: Net radiative flux at surface
Description: Net radiative flux at surface

Title: Transpiration
Description: Transpiration

Title: Surface Temperature Where Land or Sea Ice
Description: Surface temperature of all surfaces except open ocean, sampled synoptically.

Title: Hourly Temperature at Surface
Description: Hourly Temperature at 2m above the surface

Title: Hourly Temperature at Surface
Description: Hourly Temperature at 2m above the surface

Title: Change in Interception Storage
Description: change_over_time_in_canopy_water_amount

Title: Change in Groundwater
Description: change_over_time_in_groundwater

Title: Change in River Storage
Description: Change in River Storage

Title: Change in Soil Moisture
Description: Change in Soil Moisture

Title: Change in Snow Water Equivalent
Description: Change in Snow Water Equivalent

Title: Change in Surface Water Storage
Description: Change in Surface Water Storage

Title: Snow Evaporation
Description: The flux due to conversion of liquid or solid water into vapor at the surface where there is snow on land

Title: Potential Evapotranspiration
Description: water_potential_evapotranspiration_flux

Title: Daily water evaporation flux from soil
Description: Water evaporation flux from soil but for daily averages i.e., the evspsblsoi variable, which is only currently defined for monthly averages

Title: Daily water evaporation flux from canopy
Description: The same as the current variable evspsblveg but defined on daily timescales.

Title: Gross Primary Production on Vegetation type as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: The rate of synthesis of biomass from inorganic precursors by autotrophs ("producers") expressed as the mass of carbon which it contains. For example, photosynthesis in plants or phytoplankton. The producers also respire some of this biomass and the difference is referred to as the net primary production. Reported on land-use tiles.

Title: Leaf Area Index
Description: A ratio obtained by dividing the total upper leaf surface area of vegetation by the (horizontal) surface area of the land on which it grows.

Title: Leaf Area Index on Vegetation type
Description: A ratio obtained by dividing the total upper leaf surface area of vegetation by the (horizontal) surface area of the land on which it grows.

Title: Subsurface Runoff
Description: subsurface_runoff_flux

Title: Surface Runoff
Description: surface_runoff_flux

Title: Frozen Water Content of Soil Layer
Description: in each soil layer, the mass of water in ice phase.  Reported as "missing" for grid cells occupied entirely by "sea"

Title: Liquid Water Content of Soil Layer
Description: in each soil layer, the mass of water in liquid phase.  Reported as "missing" for grid cells occupied entirely by "sea"

Title: Total Water Content of Soil Layer
Description: in each soil layer, the mass of water in all phases, including ice.  Reported as "missing" for grid cells occupied entirely by "sea"

Title: Total Soil Wetness
Description: relative_soil_moisture_content_above_field_capacity

Title: Terrestrial Water Storage
Description: canopy_and_surface_and_subsurface_water_amount

Title: Net Primary Production on Vegetation type as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: "Production of carbon" means the production of biomass expressed as the mass of carbon which it contains. Net primary production is the excess of gross primary production (rate of synthesis of biomass from inorganic precursors) by autotrophs ("producers"), for example, photosynthesis in plants or phytoplankton, over the rate at which the autotrophs themselves respire some of this biomass. "Productivity" means production per unit area. The phrase "expressed_as" is used in the construction A_expressed_as_B, where B is a chemical constituent of A. It means that the quantity indicated by the standard name is calculated solely with respect to the B contained in A, neglecting all other chemical constituents of A.

Title: Groundwater Recharge from Soil Layer
Description: water_flux_from_soil_layer_to_groundwater

Title: Autotrophic Respiration on Vegetation type as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Carbon mass flux per unit area into atmosphere due to autotrophic respiration on land (respiration by producers) [see rh for heterotrophic production]. Calculated on vegetation type.

Title: Heterotrophic Respiration on Vegetation type as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Carbon mass flux per unit area into atmosphere due to heterotrophic respiration on land (respiration by consumers), calculated on vegetation type.

Title: River Inflow
Description: water_flux_to_downstream

Title: River Discharge
Description: water_flux_from_upstream

Title: Root Zone Soil Moisture
Description: water_content_of_root_zone

Title: Surface Water Storage
Description: Total liquid water storage, other than soil, snow or interception storage (i.e. lakes, river channel or depression storage).

Title: Temperature of Soil
Description: Temperature of each soil layer.  Reported as "missing" for grid cells occupied entirely by "sea".

Title: Water Table Depth
Description: depth_of_soil_moisture_saturation

Title: Thickness of Soil Layers
Description: Thickness of Soil Layers

Title: Canopy Albedo
Description: Canopy Albedo

Title: Mass of 13C in All Terrestrial Carbon Pools
Description: Carbon-13 mass content per unit area in vegetation (any living plants e.g. trees, shrubs, grass), litter (dead plant material in or above the soil), soil, and forestry and agricultural products (e.g. paper, cardboard, furniture, timber for construction, biofuels and food for both humans and livestock).

Title: Mass of 13C in Litter Pool
Description: Carbon-13 mass content per unit area litter (dead plant material in or above the soil).

Title: Mass of 13C in Soil Pool
Description: Carbon-13 mass content per unit area in soil.

Title: Mass of 13C in Vegetation
Description: Carbon-13 mass content per unit area in vegetation (any living plants e.g. trees, shrubs, grass).

Title: Mass of 14C in All Terrestrial Carbon Pools
Description: Carbon-14 mass content per unit area in vegetation (any living plants e.g. trees, shrubs, grass), litter (dead plant material in or above the soil), soil, and forestry and agricultural products (e.g. paper, cardboard, furniture, timber for construction, biofuels and food for both humans and livestock).

Title: Mass of 14C in Litter Pool
Description: Carbon-14 mass content per unit area litter (dead plant material in or above the soil).

Title: Mass of 14C in Soil Pool
Description: Carbon-14 mass content per unit area in soil.

Title: Mass of 14C in Vegetation
Description: only requested for DECK HIST

Title: Total Carbon in All Terrestrial Carbon Pools
Description: Report missing data over ocean grid cells. For fractional land report value averaged over the land fraction.

Title: Carbon Mass in Coarse Woody Debris
Description: "Content" indicates a quantity per unit area. "Wood debris" means dead organic matter composed of coarse wood. It is distinct from fine litter. The precise distinction between "fine" and "coarse" is model dependent.

Title: Carbon Mass in Below-Ground Litter
Description: subsurface litter pool fed by root inputs.

Title: Carbon Mass in Above-Ground Litter
Description: Surface or near-surface litter pool fed by leaf and above-ground litterfall

Title: Carbon Mass in Vegetation Components Other than Leaves, Stems and Roots
Description: E.g. fruits, seeds, etc.

Title: Carbon Mass in Model Soil Pool
Description: Carbon mass in the full depth of the soil model.

Title: Carbon Mass in Soil Pool Above 1m Depth
Description: Report missing data over ocean grid cells. For fractional land report value averaged over the land fraction.

Title: Carbon Mass in Each Model Soil Level (Summed over All Soil Carbon Pools in That Level)
Description: for models with vertically discretised soil carbon, report total soil carbon for each level

Title: Carbon Mass in Each Model Soil Pool (Summed over Vertical Levels)
Description: For models with multiple soil carbon pools, report each pool here. If models also have vertical discretisation these should be aggregated

Title: Carbon Mass in Stem
Description: including sapwood and hardwood.

Title: Carbon Mass in Vegetation on Grass Tiles
Description: "Content" indicates a quantity per unit area. "Vegetation" means any plants e.g. trees, shrubs, grass. Plants are autotrophs i.e. "producers" of biomass using carbon obtained from carbon dioxide.

Title: Carbon Mass in Vegetation on Shrub Tiles
Description: "Content" indicates a quantity per unit area. "Vegetation" means any plants e.g. trees, shrubs, grass. Plants are autotrophs i.e. "producers" of biomass using carbon obtained from carbon dioxide.

Title: Carbon Mass in Vegetation on Tree Tiles
Description: "Content" indicates a quantity per unit area. "Vegetation" means any plants e.g. trees, shrubs, grass. Plants are autotrophs i.e. "producers" of biomass using carbon obtained from carbon dioxide.

Title: Canopy Covered Area Percentage
Description: Canopy covered fraction

Title: Percentage Cover by C3 Crops
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell covered by C3 crops

Title: Percentage Cover by C4 Crops
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell covered by C4 crops

Title: Potential Evapotranspiration
Description: at surface; potential flux of water into the atmosphere due to conversion of both liquid and solid phases to vapor (from underlying surface and vegetation)

Title: Carbon Mass Flux from Vegetation, Litter or Soil Pools into the Atmosphere Due to any Human Activity [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Anthropogenic flux of carbon as carbon dioxide into the atmosphere. That is, emissions influenced, caused, or created by human activity. Anthropogenic emission of carbon dioxide includes fossil fuel use, cement production, agricultural burning and sources associated with anthropogenic land use change, except forest regrowth.

Title: Biological Nitrogen Fixation
Description: The fixation (uptake of nitrogen gas directly from the atmosphere) of nitrogen due to biological processes.

Title: Lateral Transfer of Carbon out of Grid Cell That Eventually Goes into Ocean
Description: leached carbon etc that goes into run off or river routing and finds its way into ocean should be reported here.

Title: Deforested Biomass That Goes into Atmosphere as a Result of Anthropogenic Land-Use Change [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: When land use change results in deforestation of natural vegetation (trees or grasslands) then natural biomass is removed. The treatment of deforested biomass differs significantly across models, but it should be straight-forward to compare deforested biomass across models.

Title: Deforested Biomass That Goes into Product Pool as a Result of Anthropogenic Land-Use Change
Description: When land use change results in deforestation of natural vegetation (trees or grasslands) then natural biomass is removed. The treatment of deforested biomass differs significantly across models, but it should be straight-forward to compare deforested biomass across models.

Title: Carbon Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to CO2 Emission from Fire Including All Sources [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: From all sources,  Including natural, anthropogenic and Land-use change. Only total fire emissions can be compared to observations.

Title: Carbon Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to CO2 Emission from Natural Fire [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: CO2 emissions from natural fires

Title: Harvested Biomass That Goes Straight into Atmosphere as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: any harvested carbon that is assumed to decompose immediately into the atmosphere is reported here

Title: Harvested Biomass That Goes into Product Pool
Description: be it food or wood harvest, any carbon that is subsequently stored is reported here

Title: Carbon Mass Flux from Litter, CWD or any non-Living Pool into Atmosphere Due to CO2 Emission from All Fire [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Required for unambiguous separation of vegetation and soil + litter turnover times, since total fire flux draws from both sources

Title: Net Carbon Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to Land-Use Change [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Net Carbon Mass Flux into Atmosphere due to Land Use Change

Title: Carbon Transferred Directly to Atmosphere Due to any Land-Use or Land-Cover Change Activities [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: This annual mean flux refers to the transfer of carbon directly to the atmosphere due to any land-use or land-cover change activities.  Include carbon transferred due to deforestation or agricultural directly into atmosphere, and  emissions form anthropogenic pools into atmosphere

Title: Total Land N2O Flux
Description: Surface upward flux of nitrous oxide (N2O) from vegetation (any living plants e.g. trees, shrubs, grass), litter (dead plant material in or above the soil), soil.

Title: Nitrogen Mass Flux out of Land Due to any Human Activity
Description: will require some careful definition to make sure we capture everything - any human activity that releases nitrogen from land  instead of into product pool goes here. E.g. Deforestation fire, harvest assumed to decompose straight away, grazing...

Title: Lateral Transfer of Nitrogen out of Grid Cell That Eventually Goes into Ocean
Description: leached nitrogen etc that goes into run off or river routing and finds its way into ocean should be reported here.

Title: Total Nitrogen Mass Flux from Litter to Soil
Description: In accordance with common usage in geophysical disciplines, "flux" implies per unit area, called "flux density" in physics. "Litter" is dead plant material in or above the soil.

Title: Total Land NOx Flux
Description: The surface called "surface" means the lower boundary of the atmosphere. "Upward" indicates a vector component which is positive when directed upward (negative downward). In accordance with common usage in geophysical disciplines, "flux" implies per unit area, called "flux density" in physics. The phrase "expressed_as" is used in the construction A_expressed_as_B, where B is a chemical constituent of A. It means that the quantity indicated by the standard name is calculated solely with respect to the B contained in A, neglecting all other chemical constituents of A. "Nox" means a combination of two radical species containing nitrogen and oxygen NO+NO2. "Vegetation" means any living plants e.g. trees, shrubs, grass. "Litter" is dead plant material in or above the soil.

Title: Deforested or Harvested Biomass as a Result of Anthropogenic Land-Use or Change
Description: When land use change results in deforestation of natural vegetation (trees or grasslands) then natural biomass is removed. The treatment of deforested biomass differs significantly across models, but it should be straight-forward to compare deforested biomass across models.

Title: Total Nitrogen Mass Flux from Vegetation to Litter
Description: In accordance with common usage in geophysical disciplines, "flux" implies per unit area, called "flux density" in physics. "Litter" is dead plant material in or above the soil. "Vegetation" means any living plants e.g. trees, shrubs, grass.

Title: Total Nitrogen Mass Flux from Vegetation Directly to Soil
Description: In some models part of nitrogen (e.g., root exudate) can go directly into the soil pool without entering litter.

Title: Dry and Wet Deposition of Reactive Nitrogen onto Land
Description: Surface deposition rate of nitrogen.

Title: Total Nitrogen Added for Cropland Fertilisation (Artificial and Manure)
Description: Total Nitrogen added for cropland fertilisation (artificial and manure). Relative to total land area of a grid cell, not relative to agricultural area

Title: Total Nitrogen Lost to the Atmosphere (Sum of NHx, NOx, N2O, N2)
Description: Total flux of Nitrogen from the land into the atmosphere.

Title: Total Nitrogen Lost to the Atmosphere (Including NHx, NOx, N2O, N2) from Fire
Description: Flux of Nitrogen from the land into the atmosphere due to fire

Title: Total Nitrogen Lost to the Atmosphere (Including NHx, NOx, N2O, N2) from All Processes Except Fire
Description: Flux of Nitrogen from the land into the atmosphere due to all processes other than fire

Title: Total Nitrogen Loss to Leaching or Runoff (Sum of Ammonium, Nitrite and Nitrate)
Description: In accordance with common usage in geophysical disciplines, "flux" implies per unit area, called "flux density" in physics. The specification of a physical process by the phrase "due_to_" process means that the quantity named is a single term in a sum of terms which together compose the general quantity named by omitting the phrase. "Leaching" means the loss of water soluble chemical species from soil. Runoff is the liquid water which drains from land. If not specified, "runoff" refers to the sum of surface runoff and subsurface drainage.

Title: Total Nitrogen Lost (Including NHx, NOx, N2O, N2 and Leaching)
Description: Not all models split losses into gaseous and leaching

Title: Net Nitrogen Release from Soil and Litter as the Outcome of Nitrogen Immobilisation and Gross Mineralisation
Description: Loss of soil nitrogen through remineralization and immobilisation. Remineralization is the degradation of organic matter into inorganic forms of carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus and other micronutrients, which consumes oxygen and releases energy. Immobilisation of nitrogen refers to retention of nitrogen by micro-organisms under certain conditions, making it unavailable for plants.

Title: Total Plant Nitrogen Uptake (Sum of Ammonium and Nitrate) Irrespective of the Source of Nitrogen
Description: The uptake of nitrogen by fixation: nitrogen fixation means the uptake of nitrogen gas directly from the atmosphere.

Title: Decomposition out of Product Pools to CO2 in Atmosphere as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Flux of CO2 from product pools into the atmosphere. Examples of "forestry and agricultural products" are paper, cardboard, furniture, timber for construction, biofuels and food for both humans and livestock. Models that simulate land use changes have one or more pools of carbon that represent these products in order to conserve carbon and allow its eventual release into the atmosphere, for example, when the products decompose in landfill sites.

Title: Carbon Mass Flux from Vegetation into Atmosphere Due to CO2 Emission from All Fire [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Required for unambiguous separation of vegetation and soil + litter turnover times, since total fire flux draws from both sources

Title: Total Carbon Mass Flux from Vegetation to Litter as a Result of Mortality
Description: needed to separate changing vegetation C turnover times resulting from changing allocation versus changing mortality

Title: Total Carbon Mass Flux from Vegetation to Litter as a Result of Leaf, Branch, and Root Senescence
Description: needed to separate changing vegetation C turnover times resulting from changing allocation versus changing mortality

Title: Total Carbon Mass Flux from Vegetation to Soil as a Result of Mortality
Description: needed to separate changing vegetation C turnover times resulting from changing allocation versus changing mortality

Title: Total Carbon Mass Flux from Vegetation to Soil as a Result of Leaf, Branch, and Root Senescence
Description: needed to separate changing vegetation C turnover times resulting from changing allocation versus changing mortality

Title: Percentage of Grid Cell for Each Land-Use Tile
Description: End of month values (not monthly mean); note that percentage should be reported as percentage of land grid cell (example: frac_lnd = 0.5, frac_ocn = 0.5, frac_crop_lnd = 0.2 (of land portion of grid cell), then frac_lut(crop) = 0.5\*0.2 = 0.1)

Title: Gross Primary Production on Grass Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total GPP of grass in the grid cell

Title: Gross Primary Production on Land-Use Tile as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: The rate of synthesis of biomass from inorganic precursors by autotrophs ("producers") expressed as the mass of carbon which it contains. For example, photosynthesis in plants or phytoplankton. The producers also respire some of this biomass and the difference is referred to as the net primary production. Reported on land-use tiles.

Title: Gross Primary Production on Shrub Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total GPP of shrubs in the grid cell

Title: Gross Primary Production on Tree Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total GPP of trees in the grid cell

Title: Carbon-13 Mass Flux out of Atmosphere Due to Gross Primary Production on Land [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: The rate of synthesis of carbon-13 in biomass from inorganic precursors by autotrophs ("producers") expressed as the mass of carbon which it contains. For example, photosynthesis in plants or phytoplankton. The producers also respire some of this biomass and the difference is referred to as the net primary production.

Title: Carbon-14 Mass Flux out of Atmosphere Due to Gross Primary Production on Land [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: The rate of synthesis of carbon-14 in biomass from inorganic precursors by autotrophs ("producers") expressed as the mass of carbon which it contains. For example, photosynthesis in plants or phytoplankton. The producers also respire some of this biomass and the difference is referred to as the net primary production.

Title: C3 Natural Grass Area Percentage
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell covered by C3 natural grass.

Title: C4 Natural Grass Area Percentage
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell covered by C4 natural grass.

Title: Latent Heat Flux on Land-Use Tile
Description: The surface called "surface" means the lower boundary of the atmosphere. "Upward" indicates a vector component which is positive when directed upward (negative downward). The surface latent heat flux is the exchange of heat between the surface and the air on account of evaporation (including sublimation). In accordance with common usage in geophysical disciplines, "flux" implies per unit area, called "flux density" in physics.

Title: Sensible Heat Flux on Land-Use Tile
Description: Upward sensible heat flux on land use tiles. The surface sensible heat flux, also called turbulent heat flux, is the exchange of heat between the surface and the air by motion of air.

Title: Irrigation Flux Including any Irrigation for Crops, Trees, Pasture, or Urban Lawns
Description: Mass flux of water due to irrigation.

Title: Leaf Area Index on Land-Use Tile
Description: A ratio obtained by dividing the total upper leaf surface area of vegetation by the (horizontal) surface area of the land on which it grows.

Title: Frozen Water Content of Soil Layer
Description: in each soil layer, the mass of water in ice phase.  Reported as "missing" for grid cells occupied entirely by "sea"

Title: Liquid Water Content of Soil Layer
Description: in each soil layer, the mass of water in liquid phase.  Reported as "missing" for grid cells occupied entirely by "sea"

Title: Total Water Content of Soil Layer
Description: in each soil layer, the mass of water in all phases, including ice.  Reported as "missing" for grid cells occupied entirely by "sea"

Title: Moisture in Upper Portion of Soil Column of Land-Use Tile
Description: the mass of water in all phases in a thin surface layer; integrate over uppermost 10cm

Title: Terrestrial Water Storage
Description: requested for C4MIP, OCMIP/OMIP, LUMIP, ScenarioMIP, DECK, DAMIP, GeoMIP, LS3MIP, ??

Title: Total Nitrogen in All Terrestrial Nitrogen Pools
Description: Report missing data over ocean grid cells. For fractional land report value averaged over the land fraction.

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Leaves
Description: "Content" indicates a quantity per unit area.

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Litter Pool
Description: Report missing data over ocean grid cells. For fractional land report value averaged over the land fraction.

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Coarse Woody Debris
Description: "Content" indicates a quantity per unit area. "Wood debris" means dead organic matter composed of coarse wood. It is distinct from fine litter. The precise distinction between "fine" and "coarse" is model dependent. The sum of the quantities with standard names wood_debris_mass_content_of_nitrogen, surface_litter_mass_content_of_nitrogen and subsurface_litter_mass_content_of_nitrogen is the total nitrogen mass content of dead plant material.

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Below-Ground Litter (non CWD)
Description: "Content" indicates a quantity per unit area. "Litter" is dead plant material in or above the soil. It is distinct from coarse wood debris. The precise distinction between "fine" and "coarse" is model dependent. "Subsurface litter" means the part of the litter mixed within the soil below the surface. The sum of the quantities with standard names wood_debris_mass_content_of_nitrogen, surface_litter_mass_content_of_nitrogen and subsurface_litter_mass_content_of_nitrogen is the total nitrogen mass content of dead plant material.

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Above-Ground Litter (non CWD)
Description: "Content" indicates a quantity per unit area. "Litter" is dead plant material in or above the soil. It is distinct from coarse wood debris. The precise distinction between "fine" and "coarse" is model dependent. "Surface litter" means the part of the litter resting above the soil surface. The sum of the quantities with standard names wood_debris_mass_content_of_nitrogen, surface_litter_mass_content_of_nitrogen and subsurface_litter_mass_content_of_nitrogen is the total nitrogen mass content of dead plant material.

Title: Mineral Nitrogen in the Soil
Description: SUM of ammonium, nitrite, nitrate, etc over all soil layers

Title: Mineral Ammonium in the Soil
Description: SUM of ammonium over all soil layers

Title: Mineral Nitrate in the Soil
Description: SUM of nitrate over all soil layers

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Vegetation Components Other than Leaves, Stem and Root
Description: E.g. fruits, seeds, etc.

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Products of Land-Use Change
Description: Report missing data over ocean grid cells. For fractional land report value averaged over the land fraction.

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Roots
Description: including fine and coarse roots.

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Soil Pool
Description: Report missing data over ocean grid cells. For fractional land report value averaged over the land fraction.

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Stem
Description: including sapwood and hardwood.

Title: Nitrogen Mass in Vegetation
Description: Report missing data over ocean grid cells. For fractional land report value averaged over the land fraction.

Title: Net Carbon Mass Flux into Land-Use Tile [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Computed as npp minus heterotrophic respiration minus fire minus C leaching minus harvesting/clearing. Positive rate is into the land, negative rate is from the land.  Do not include fluxes from anthropogenic product pools to atmosphere

Title: Net Carbon Mass Flux out of Atmosphere Due to Net Ecosystem Productivity on Land [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Net Ecosystem Exchange

Title: Net Mass Flux of 13C Between Atmosphere and Land (Positive into Land) as a Result of All Processes [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Flux of carbon 31as carbon dioxide into the land. This flux should be reproducible by differencing the sum of all carbon pools (cVeg, cLitter, cSoil, and cProducts or equivalently cLand) from one time step to the next, except in the case of lateral transfer of carbon due to harvest, riverine transport of dissolved organic and/or inorganic carbon, or any other process (in which case the lateral_carbon_transfer_over_land term, see below, will be zero data).-

Title: Net Mass Flux of 14C Between Atmosphere and Land (Positive into Land) as a Result of All Processes [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Flux of carbon-14 as carbon dioxide into the land. This flux should be reproducible by differencing the sum of all carbon pools (cVeg, cLitter, cSoil, and cProducts or equivalently cLand) from one time step to the next, except in the case of lateral transfer of carbon due to harvest, riverine transport of dissolved organic and/or inorganic carbon, or any other process (in which case the lateral_carbon_transfer_over_land term, see below, will be zero data).

Title: Net Primary Production on Grass Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total NPP of grass in the grid cell

Title: Net Primary Production on Land-Use Tile as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: "Production of carbon" means the production of biomass expressed as the mass of carbon which it contains. Net primary production is the excess of gross primary production (rate of synthesis of biomass from inorganic precursors) by autotrophs ("producers"), for example, photosynthesis in plants or phytoplankton, over the rate at which the autotrophs themselves respire some of this biomass. "Productivity" means production per unit area. The phrase "expressed_as" is used in the construction A_expressed_as_B, where B is a chemical constituent of A. It means that the quantity indicated by the standard name is calculated solely with respect to the B contained in A, neglecting all other chemical constituents of A.

Title: Net Primary Production Allocated to Other Pools (not Leaves Stem or Roots) as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: added for completeness with npp_root

Title: Net Primary Production on Shrub Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total NPP of shrubs in the grid cell

Title: Net Primary Production Allocated to Stem as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: added for completeness with npp_root

Title: Net Primary Production on Tree Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total NPP of trees in the grid cell

Title: C3 Pasture Area Percentage
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell covered by C3 pasture

Title: C4 Pasture Area Percentage
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell covered by C4 pasture

Title: Autotrophic Respiration on Grass Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total RA of grass in the grid cell

Title: Total Respiration from Leaves as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: added for completeness with Ra_root

Title: Autotrophic Respiration on Land-Use Tile as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Carbon mass flux per unit area into atmosphere due to autotrophic respiration on land (respiration by producers) [see rh for heterotrophic production]. Calculated on land-use tiles.

Title: Total Respiration from Other Pools (not Leaves Stem or Roots) as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: added for completeness with Ra_root

Title: Total Respiration from Roots as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total autotrophic respiration from all belowground plant parts.  This has benchmarking value because the sum of Rh and root respiration can be compared to observations of total soil respiration.

Title: Autotrophic Respiration on Shrub Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total RA of shrubs in the grid cell

Title: Total Respiration from Stem as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: added for completeness with Ra_root

Title: Autotrophic Respiration on Tree Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total RA of trees in the grid cell

Title: Carbon-13 Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to Autotrophic (Plant) Respiration on Land [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Flux of carbon-13 into the atmosphere due to plant respiration. Plant respiration is the sum of respiration by parts of plants both above and below the soil. It is assumed that all the respired carbon dioxide is emitted to the atmosphere.

Title: Carbon-14 Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to Autotrophic (Plant) Respiration on Land [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Flux of carbon-14 into the atmosphere due to plant respiration. Plant respiration is the sum of respiration by parts of plants both above and below the soil. It is assumed that all the respired carbon dioxide is emitted to the atmosphere.

Title: Heterotrophic Respiration on Grass Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total RH of grass in the grid cell

Title: Carbon Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to Heterotrophic Respiration from Litter on Land
Description: Needed to calculate litter bulk turnover time. Includes respiration from CWD as well.

Title: Heterotrophic Respiration on Land-Use Tile as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Carbon mass flux per unit area into atmosphere due to heterotrophic respiration on land (respiration by consumers), calculated on land-use tiles.

Title: Heterotrophic Respiration on Shrub Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total RH of shrubs in the grid cell

Title: Carbon Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to Heterotrophic Respiration from Soil on Land
Description: Needed to calculate soil bulk turnover time

Title: Heterotrophic Respiration on Tree Tiles as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Total RH of trees in the grid cell

Title: Carbon-13 Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to Heterotrophic Respiration on Land [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Heterotrophic respiration is respiration by heterotrophs ("consumers"), which are organisms (including animals and decomposers) that consume other organisms or dead organic material, rather than synthesising organic material from inorganic precursors using energy from the environment (especially sunlight) as autotrophs ("producers") do. Heterotrophic respiration goes on within both the soil and litter pools.

Title: Carbon-14 Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to Heterotrophic Respiration on Land [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Heterotrophic respiration is respiration by heterotrophs ("consumers"), which are organisms (including animals and decomposers) that consume other organisms or dead organic material, rather than synthesising organic material from inorganic precursors using energy from the environment (especially sunlight) as autotrophs ("producers") do. Heterotrophic respiration goes on within both the soil and litter pools.

Title: Surface Downwelling Shortwave Radiation over Land
Description: Surface Downwelling Shortwave Radiation over land. Can be used for computation of surface albedo.

Title: Surface Downwelling Shortwave Radiation over Snow
Description: Surface Downwelling Shortwave Radiation over the portion of a land grid cell covered by snow but not by ice. Can be used for computation of surface albedo.

Title: Surface Upwelling Shortwave Radiation over Land
Description: Surface Upwelling Shortwave Radiation over land. Can be used for computation of surface albedo.

Title: Surface Upwelling Shortwave Radiation over Snow
Description: Surface Upwelling Shortwave Radiation over the portion of a land grid cell covered by snow. Can be used for computation of surface albedo.

Title: Snow Water Equivalent on Land-Use Tile
Description: The surface called "surface" means the lower boundary of the atmosphere. "lwe" means liquid water equivalent. "Amount" means mass per unit area. The construction lwe_thickness_of_X_amount or _content means the vertical extent of a layer of liquid water having the same mass per unit area. Surface amount refers to the amount on the ground, excluding that on the plant or vegetation canopy.

Title: Near-Surface Air Temperature on Land Use Tile
Description: Air temperature is the bulk temperature of the air, not the surface (skin) temperature.

Title: Broadleaf Deciduous Tree Area Percentage
Description: This is the percentage of the entire grid cell that is covered by broadleaf deciduous trees.

Title: Broadleaf Evergreen Tree Area Percentage
Description: This is the percentage of the entire grid cell that is covered by broadleaf evergreen trees.

Title: Needleleaf Deciduous Tree Area Percentage
Description: This is the percentage of the entire grid cell that is covered by needleleaf deciduous trees.

Title: Needleleaf Evergreen Tree Area Percentage
Description: This is the percentage of the entire grid cell that is covered by needleleaf evergreen trees.

Title: Surface Temperature on Landuse Tile
Description: Surface temperature (i.e. temperature at which long-wave radiation emitted)

Title: Total Vegetated Percentage Cover
Description: Percentage of grid cell that is covered by vegetation.This SHOULD be the sum of tree, grass (natural and pasture), crop and shrub fractions.

Title: Height of the Vegetation Canopy
Description: Vegetation height averaged over all vegetation types and over the vegetated fraction of a grid cell.

Title: Height of Grass
Description: Vegetation height averaged over the grass fraction of a grid cell.

Title: Height of Shrubs
Description: Vegetation height averaged over the shrub fraction of a grid cell.

Title: Height of Trees
Description: Vegetation height averaged over the tree fraction of a grid cell.

Title: Grid Averaged Methane Emissions from Wetlands
Description: Net upward flux of methane (NH4) from wetlands (areas where water covers the soil, or is present either at or near the surface of the soil all year or for varying periods of time during the year, including during the growing season).

Title: Grid Averaged Methane Consumption (Methanotrophy) from Wetlands
Description: Biological consumption (methanotrophy) of methane (NH4) by wetlands (areas where water covers the soil, or is present either at or near the surface of the soil all year or for varying periods of time during the year, including during the growing season).

Title: Grid Averaged Methane Production (Methanogenesis) from Wetlands
Description: Biological emissions (methanogenesis) of methane (NH4) from wetlands (areas where water covers the soil, or is present either at or near the surface of the soil all year or for varying periods of time during the year, including during the growing season).

Title: Wetland Percentage Cover
Description: Percentage of grid cell covered by wetland. Report only one year if  fixed percentage is used, or time series if values are determined dynamically.

Title: Bare Soil Percentage Area Coverage
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell  that is covered by bare soil.

Title: Carbon  in Above and Below-Ground Litter Pools on Land-Use Tiles
Description: end of year values (not annual mean)

Title: Wood and Agricultural Product Pool Carbon Associated with Land-Use Tiles
Description: Anthropogenic pools associated with land use tiles into which harvests and cleared carbon are deposited before release into atmosphere PLUS any remaining anthropogenic pools that may be associated with lands which were converted into land use tiles during reported period. Examples of products include paper, cardboard, timber for construction, and crop harvest for food or fuel. Does NOT include residue which is deposited into soil or litter; end of year values (not annual mean).

Title: Carbon in Soil Pool on Land-Use Tiles
Description: end of year values (not annual mean)

Title: Carbon in Vegetation on Land-Use Tiles
Description: end of year values (not annual mean)

Title: Percentage Crop Cover
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell  that is covered by crop.

Title: Annual Gross Percentage That Was Transferred into This Tile from Other Land-Use Tiles
Description: Cumulative percentage transitions over the year; note that percentage should be reported as a percentage of atmospheric grid cell

Title: Percentage of Grid Cell for Each Land-Use Tile
Description: End of year values (not annual mean); note that percentage should be reported as percentage of land grid cell (example: frac_lnd = 0.5, frac_ocn = 0.5, frac_crop_lnd = 0.2 (of land portion of grid cell), then frac_lut(crop) = 0.5\*0.2 = 0.1)

Title: Annual Gross Percentage of Land-Use Tile That Was Transferred into Other Land-Use Tiles
Description: Cumulative percentage transitions over the year; note that percentage should be reported as percentage of atmospheric grid cell

Title: Natural Grass Area Percentage
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell that is covered by natural grass.

Title: Percentage of Grid Cell That Is Land but neither Vegetation Covered nor Bare Soil
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell  that is land and is covered by  neither vegetation nor bare-soil (e.g., urban, ice, lakes, etc.)

Title: Percentage Cover by Shrub
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell  that is covered by shrub.

Title: Tree Cover Percentage
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell  that is covered by trees.

Title: Total Vegetated Percentage Cover
Description: Percentage of grid cell that is covered by vegetation.This SHOULD be the sum of tree, grass (natural and pasture), crop and shrub fractions.

Title: Grid-Cell Area for Ice Sheet Variables
Description: Cell areas any grid used to report ice sheet variables. These cell areas should be defined to enable exact calculation of area integrals (e.g., of vertical fluxes of energy at the surface and top of the atmosphere)

Title: Grid-Cell Area for Ice Sheet Variables
Description: Cell areas any grid used to report ice sheet variables. These cell areas should be defined to enable exact calculation of area integrals (e.g., of vertical fluxes of energy at the surface and top of the atmosphere)

Title: Surface Altitude
Description: The surface called "surface" means the lower boundary of the atmosphere. Altitude is the (geometric) height above the geoid, which is the reference geopotential surface. The geoid is similar to mean sea level.

Title: Surface Altitude
Description: The surface called "surface" means the lower boundary of the atmosphere. Altitude is the (geometric) height above the geoid, which is the reference geopotential surface. The geoid is similar to mean sea level.

Title: Surface Altitude
Description: This is needed in case the ice sheet elevation changes in time

Title: Land Ice Area Percentage
Description: This is needed in case the ice sheet area changes in time

Title: Surface Altitude
Description: This is needed in case the ice sheet elevation changes in time

Title: Land Ice Area Percentage
Description: This is needed in case the ice sheet area changes in time

Title: Land Ice Area Percentage
Description: Percentage of grid cell covered by land ice (ice sheet, ice shelf, ice cap, glacier)

Title: Bare Soil Percentage Area Coverage
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell  that is covered by bare soil.

Title: Percentage of Entire Grid Cell That Is Covered by Burnt Vegetation (All Classes)
Description: Percentage of grid cell burned due to all fires including natural and anthropogenic fires and those associated with anthropogenic land use change

Title: Percentage Cover by C3 Plant Functional Type
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell  that is covered by C3 PFTs (including grass, crops, and trees).

Title: Percentage Cover by C4 Plant Functional Type
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell  that is covered by C4 PFTs (including grass and crops).

Title: Carbon Mass in Geologic Storage
Description: Mass of carbon that has been intentionally sequestered in geologic storage. The definition of geologic storage here is that it be stored for periods of time that are long as compared to the simulation.

Title: Carbon Mass in Leaves
Description: Carbon mass per unit area in leaves.

Title: Carbon Mass in Litter Pool
Description: "Litter" is dead plant material in or above the soil. It is distinct from coarse wood debris. The precise distinction between "fine" and "coarse" is model dependent. "Content" indicates a quantity per unit area. The sum of the quantities with standard names surface_litter_mass_content_of_carbon and subsurface_litter_mass_content_of_carbon has the standard name litter_mass_content_of_carbon.

Title: Carbon Mass in Products of Land-Use Change
Description: Carbon mass per unit area in that has been removed from the environment through  land use change.

Title: Carbon Mass in Roots
Description: including fine and coarse roots.

Title: Carbon Mass in Vegetation
Description: Carbon mass per unit area in vegetation.

Title: Percentage Crop Cover
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell  that is covered by crop.

Title: Water Evaporation from Soil
Description: includes sublimation.

Title: Evaporation from Canopy
Description: the canopy evaporation+sublimation (if present in model).

Title: Carbon Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to CO2 Emission from Fire Excluding Land-Use Change [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: CO2 emissions (expressed as a carbon mass flux) from natural fires + human ignition fires as calculated by the fire module of the DGVM, but excluding any CO2 flux from fire included in fLuc, defined below (CO2 Flux to Atmosphere from Land Use Change).

Title: Harvested Biomass That Goes into Geological Storage
Description: Flux of carbon harvested from biomass that goes into geologic storage for the purposes of intentional carbon dioxide removal, via efforts such as bioenergy with carbon capture and storage (BECCS) or biomass removal and storage (BiCRS). The definition of geologic storage here is that the resulting carbon be  stored for a period of time that is long relative to that of the simulation.

Title: Total Carbon Mass Flux from Litter to Soil
Description: Carbon mass flux per unit area into soil from litter (dead plant material in or above the soil).

Title: Total Carbon Mass Flux from Vegetation to Litter
Description: In accordance with common usage in geophysical disciplines, "flux" implies per unit area, called "flux density" in physics. "Vegetation" means any living plants e.g. trees, shrubs, grass. "Litter" is dead plant material in or above the soil. It is distinct from coarse wood debris. The precise distinction between "fine" and "coarse" is model dependent. The sum of the quantities with standard names mass_flux_of_carbon_into_litter_from_vegetation_due_to_mortality and mass_flux_of_carbon_into_litter_from_vegetation_due_to_senescence is mass_flux_of_carbon_into_litter_from_vegetation.

Title: Total Carbon Mass Flux from Vegetation Directly to Soil
Description: In some models part of carbon (e.g., root exudate) can go directly into the soil pool without entering litter.

Title: Carbon Mass Flux out of Atmosphere Due to Gross Primary Production on Land [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: The rate of synthesis of biomass from inorganic precursors by autotrophs ("producers") expressed as the mass of carbon which it contains. For example, photosynthesis in plants or phytoplankton. The producers also respire some of this biomass and the difference is referred to as the net primary production.

Title: Natural Grass Area Percentage
Description: Percentage of entire grid cell that is covered by natural grass.

Title: Leaf Area Index
Description: A ratio obtained by dividing the total upper leaf surface area of vegetation by the (horizontal) surface area of the land on which it grows.

Title: Percentage of Area by Vegetation or Land-Cover Category
Description: The categories may differ from model to model, depending on their  PFT definitions.   This may include natural PFTs, anthropogenic PFTs, bare soil, lakes, urban areas, etc.   Sum of all should equal the fraction of the grid-cell that is land.

Title: Total Runoff
Description: the total runoff (including "drainage" through the base of the soil model) leaving the land portion of the grid cell.

Title: Surface Runoff
Description: the total surface runoff leaving the land portion of the grid cell.

Title: Total Soil Moisture Content
Description: the mass per unit area  (summed over all soil layers) of water in all phases.

Title: Moisture in Upper Portion of Soil Column
Description: the mass of water in all phases in a thin surface soil layer.

Title: Carbon Mass Flux out of Atmosphere Due to Net Biospheric Production on Land [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: This is the net mass flux of carbon between land and atmosphere calculated as photosynthesis MINUS the sum of  plant and soil respiration, carbonfluxes  from fire, harvest, grazing  and land use change. Positive flux  is into the land.

Title: Net Primary Production on Land as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: "Production of carbon" means the production of biomass expressed as the mass of carbon which it contains. Net primary production is the excess of gross primary production (rate of synthesis of biomass from inorganic precursors) by autotrophs ("producers"), for example, photosynthesis in plants or phytoplankton, over the rate at which the autotrophs themselves respire some of this biomass. "Productivity" means production per unit area. The phrase "expressed_as" is used in the construction A_expressed_as_B, where B is a chemical constituent of A. It means that the quantity indicated by the standard name is calculated solely with respect to the B contained in A, neglecting all other chemical constituents of A.

Title: Net Primary Production Allocated to Leaves as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: This is the rate of carbon uptake by leaves due to NPP

Title: Net Primary Production Allocated to Roots as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: This is the rate of carbon uptake by roots due to NPP

Title: Percentage of Land Which Is Anthropogenic Pasture
Description: fraction of entire grid cell  that is covered by anthropogenic pasture.

Title: Precipitation onto Canopy
Description: the precipitation flux that is intercepted by the vegetation canopy (if present in model) before reaching the ground.

Title: Carbon Mass Flux into Atmosphere Due to Autotrophic (Plant) Respiration on Land [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Carbon mass flux per unit area into atmosphere due to autotrophic respiration on land (respiration by producers) [see rh for heterotrophic production]

Title: Percentage of Grid Cell That Is Land but neither Vegetation Covered nor Bare Soil
Description: fraction of entire grid cell  that is land and is covered by "non-vegetation" and "non-bare-soil" (e.g., urban, ice, lakes, etc.)

Title: Total Heterotrophic Respiration on Land as Carbon Mass Flux [kgC m-2 s-1]
Description: Carbon mass flux per unit area into atmosphere due to heterotrophic respiration on land (respiration by consumers)

Title: Percentage Cover by Shrub
Description: fraction of entire grid cell  that is covered by shrub.

Title: Transpiration
Description: Transpiration (may include dew formation as a negative flux).

Title: Tree Cover Percentage
Description: fraction of entire grid cell  that is covered by trees.

Title: Temperature of Soil
Description: Temperature of each soil layer.  Reported as "missing" for grid cells occupied entirely by "sea".

Title: irrigation water demand
Description: the total amount of irrigation water demand

Title: irrigation water withdrawal from groundwater
Description: the amount of water withdrawal for irrigation from ground water, including deep soil water, confined and unconfined aquifer, etc

Title: irrigation water withdrawal
Description: the total amount of water withdrawal from multiple sources

Title: irrigation water withdrawal from surface water
Description: the amount of water withdrawal for irrigation from surface water, including rivers, lakes, reservoirs, etc.)

Title: Total Runoff
Description: computed as the total runoff (including "drainage" through the base of the soil model) leaving the land portion of the grid cell divided by the land area in the grid cell.

Title: Total Soil Moisture Content
Description: the mass per unit area  (summed over all soil layers) of water in all phases.

Title: Moisture in Upper Portion of Soil Column
Description: the mass of water in all phases in a thin surface soil layer.

Title: Surface Temperature Where Land or Sea Ice
Description: Surface temperature of all surfaces except open ocean.

Title: Grid-Cell Area for River Model Variables
Description: Cell areas for any grid used to report river model variables (may be the same as for atmospheric variables). These cell areas should be defined to enable exact calculation of area integrals (e.g., of vertical fluxes of energy at the surface and top of the atmosphere).

Title: Depth of Lake Below the Surface
Description: Depth of lakes, if this quantity is present in the model. If computed via volume and area, then this is lake volume divided by lake area.

Title: Total (Cumulative) Thickness of All Soil Layers
Description: Total (cumulative) thickness of all soil layers. This is the sum of individual thicknesses of all soil layers.

Title: Capacity of Soil to Store Water (Field Capacity)
Description: reported "where land": divide the total water holding capacity of all the soil in the grid cell by the land area in the grid cell;  reported as "missing" where the land fraction is 0.

Title: Surface Altitude
Description: height above the geoid; as defined here, "the geoid" is a surface of constant geopotential that, if the ocean were at rest, would coincide with mean sea level. Under this definition, the geoid changes as the mean volume of the ocean changes (e.g., due to glacial melt, or global warming of the ocean).  Reported here is the height above the present-day geoid (0.0 over ocean).

Title: Surface Altitude
Description: height above the geoid; as defined here, "the geoid" is a surface of constant geopotential that, if the ocean were at rest, would coincide with mean sea level. Under this definition, the geoid changes as the mean volume of the ocean changes (e.g., due to glacial melt, or global warming of the ocean).  Reported here is the height above the present-day geoid (0.0 over ocean).

Title: Maximum Root Depth
Description: report the maximum soil depth reachable by plant roots (if defined in model), i.e., the maximum soil depth from which they can extract moisture; report as "missing" where the land fraction is 0.

Title: Land Ice Area Percentage
Description: fraction of grid cell occupied by "permanent" ice (i.e., glaciers).

Title: Fraction of the Grid Cell Occupied by Lake
Description: Fraction of horizontal land grid cell area occupied by lake.
